summary: Uneven first set, strong second half with Box of Rain & Wharf Rat
blurb: The show opens unevenly but gains steam after Shakedown Street, delivering solid performances of Box of Rain, Wang Dang Doodle and a soaring Space > Wharf Rat transition. Jerry’s playing fluctuates, shining on Wharf Rat and Jack‑A‑Roe while Phil anchors the band with steady bass. Highlights include an energetic second set and a heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore.
review: On June 25, 1995 at RFK Stadium the Dead gave fans a night of mixed fortunes that ultimately leans toward a memorable second half. The first set stumbled out of the gate – reviewers note a flat Shakedown Street opener and an uneven early flow – but moments like Jack‑A‑Roe and a gritty Loose Lucy kept things moving. By the second set the band was clearly in its element: Phil’s bass underpinned a thunderous Box of Rain, Wang Dang Doodle and Samson & Delilah while Bill Kreutzmann laid down a solid groove that carried the group through an expansive Space → Wharf Rat segue into a long, emotive NFA. Jerry’s guitar work shows signs of decline in places, yet his vocals on Wharf Rat and his solo on Jack‑A‑Roe sparkle, and the encore’s Brokedown Palace delivers the heartfelt close fans love. Overall, despite some uneven moments, the concert offers enough high‑voltage jams and classic Dead storytelling to make it a worthwhile listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise standout moments (Box of Rain, Space > Wharf Rat, strong second‑set jams) while a few note Jerry's uneven playing and early‑set flatness; overall sentiment tilts slightly positive.

best_recording.reason: Clean soundboard capture with clear, balanced mix; includes the full show and showcases the strong second‑set moments despite minor static.

key_highlights: High‑voltage Shakedown Street opener that kicks the night into gear
Space > Wharf Rat transition leading into a long, emotive NFA
Box of Rain and Wang Dang Doodle showcase tight rhythm section and Phil’s steady bass
Heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore closes the show on an emotional note

band_performance.Jerry: Playing is uneven – early set feels flat, but he delivers strong vocals on Wharf Rat and solid solos on Jack‑A‑Roe; moments of inspired guitar appear later despite noted decline.
band_performance.Phil: Provides steady, reliable bass throughout; especially praised on Box of Rain where his line anchors the band.
band_performance.Bob: Keeps vocal presence clear (e.g., Truckin') and adds energetic stage dynamics in the latter part of the concert.
band_performance.Mickey: Lays down solid percussion foundation, complementing Bill's tight drumming; no specific critiques noted.
band_performance.Billy: Earns compliments for great work on the second set, keeping the rhythm tight and driving the jams forward.
